title: The One with the Candy Hearts

lines.text: [Scene: Central Perk, Ross is eyeing a beautiful woman at the counter, and Joey and Chandler are egging him on to go talk to her. No pun intended. I mean it.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: I'm tellin' you Ross, she wants you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: She barely knows me. We just live in the same building.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Any contact?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: She lent me an egg once.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: You're in!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Aw, right.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.english: Hi, Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Hey. (stutters something incoherent)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Come on, Ross, you gotta get back in the game here, ok? The Rachel thing's not happening, your ex-wife is a lesbian—I don't think we need a third...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Excuse me, could we get an egg over here, still in the shell? Thanks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: An egg?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Yeah, you're gonna go up to her and say, "Here's your egg back, I'm returning your egg."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: I think it's winning.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: I think it's insane.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: She'll love it. Go with the egg, my friend.

lines.text: (Ross walks over to the woman, egg in hand.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Think it'll work?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: No, it's suicide. The man's got an egg.

lines.text: [Scene: Central Perk, Monica, Rachel, Phoebe, Joey, Chandler are there. Ross is still talking to the beautiful woman.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: You can not do this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Do what, do what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Roger wants to take her out tomorrow night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: No! Phoebes! Don't you remember why you dumped the guy?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: 'Cause he was creepy, and mean, and a little frightening... alright, still, it's nice to have a date on Valentine's Day!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: But Phoebe, you can go out with a creepy guy any night of the year. I know I do.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Well, what are you guys doing tomorrow night?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Actually, tomorrow night kinda depends on how tonight goes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, uh, listen, about tonight...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: No, no, no, don't you dare bail on me. The only reason she's goin' out with me is because I said I could bring a friend for her friend.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Yes, I know, but her friend sounds like such a...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Pathetic mess? I know, but—come on, man, she's needy, she's vulnerable. I'm thinkin', cha-ching! (Rachel throws a roll at Joey. He picks it up and eats it.) Thanks. Look, you have not been out with a woman since Janice. You're doin' this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Hi. She said yes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Yes! Way to go, man! (Chandler and Ross hug. Something crunches in Ross' shirt pocket.) Still got the egg, huh?

lines.text: [Scene: A Restaurant, Joey and Chandler are there, waiting for their dates to show up.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (Looking at himself in the reflection on a knife) How do I look?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, uh, I... don't... care. (Joey's date shows up) Ok, now, remember, no trading. You get the pretty one, I get the mess.

lines.speaker: Lorraine
lines.english: Hi, Joey. Well well, look what you brought. Very nice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: ...And what did you bring?

lines.speaker: Lorraine
lines.english: She's checking the coats. Joey, I'm gonna go wash the cab smell off my hands. Will you get me a white Zinfandel, and a glass of red for Janice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Janice?

lines.text: (Lorraine leaves. Joey shakes his head as though to say, 'It can't be the same Janice.' Janice enters.)

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Oh.... my.... God.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (angrily) Hey, it's Janice.

lines.text: [Scene: The bathroom at the restaurant, Chandler and Joey are talking.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Ok, I'm makin' a break for it, I'm goin' out the window.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: No, no, no, don't! I've been waitin' for like, forever to go out with Lorraine. Just calm down.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Calm down? Calm down? You set me up with the woman that I've dumped twice in the last five months!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (at the urinal) Can you stop yellin'? You're makin' me nervous, and I can't go when I'm nervous.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: I'm sorry, I'm sorry, you're right. (gets up right behind Joey and yells in his ear) Come on, do it, do it, go, come on!!!

lines.text: [Scene: Monica and Rachel's, the girls are all there, discussing their bad luck with men.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Ok, ok, Roger was creepy, but he was nothing compared to Pete Carney.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Which one was Pete Carney?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Pete the Weeper? Remember that guy who used to cry every time we had sex. (imitating) "Was it good for you?"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Yeah, well, I'll take a little crying any day over Howard-the-"I-win"-guy. (imitating) "I win! I win!" I went out with the guy for two months—I didn't get to win once.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: How did we end up with these jerks? We're good people!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I don't know. Maybe we're some kinda magnets.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: I know I am. That's why I can't wear a digital watch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: There's more beer, right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh! You know my friend Abby who shaves her head? She said that if you want to break the bad boyfriend cycle, you can do like a cleansing ritual.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Pheebes, this woman is voluntarily bald.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Yeah. So, we can do it tomorrow night, you guys. It's Valentine's Day. It's perfect.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Ok, well, what kind of ritual?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Ok. We can, um, we can burn the stuff they gave us.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Or?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Or...or we can chant and dance around naked, you know, with sticks.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Burning's good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Burning's good. Yeah, I got stuff to burn.

lines.text: [Scene: The Restaurant, Joey, Lorraine, Chandler, and Janice are at the table. Joey and Lorraine are seated very close, Chandler and Janice have backed their chairs away from one another.]

lines.speaker: Lorraine
lines.english: You know, ever since I was little, I've been able to pick up quarters with my toes.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Good for you. (jumps suddenly) Uh, quarters or rolls of quarters?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: By the way, Chandler. I cut you out of all my pictures. So if you want, I have a bag with just your heads.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: That's OK.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Oh, are you sure? Really? Because you know, you could make little puppets out of them, and you could use them in your theater of cruelty.

lines.text: (Lorraine whispers into Joey's ear.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (to Lorraine) We can't do that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (disgusted) What? What can't you do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Uh, can I talk to you for a second, over there?

lines.text: (Chandler and Joey leave the table.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Uh, we might be leaving now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Tell me it's "you and me" we.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: She said she wants to slather my body with stuff and then lick it off. I'm not even sure what slathering is, but I definitely want to be a part of it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Ok, you can not do this to me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: You're right, I'm sorry. You're right.

lines.speaker: Lorraine
lines.english: (to waiter) Uh, can we have three chocolate mousses to go please?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: I'm outta here. Here's my credit card. Dinner's on me. I'm sorry, Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: I hope she throws up on you.

lines.text: (Joey leaves with Lorraine. Chandler sits back down with Janice.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: So...

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Just us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, what a crappy night!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Although, I have enjoyed the fact that, uh your shirt's been stickin' outta your zipper ever since you came back from the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Excuse me. (gets up, jumps up and down while he zips his zipper up... other patrons look at him) How ya doin'?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: So, do we have the best friends or what?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Joey's not a friend. He's...a stupid man who left us his credit card. Another drink? Some dessert? A big screen TV?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: I will go for that drink.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: You got it. Good woman! (the waiter turns around, it's a man) Could we get a bottle of your most overpriced champagne?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Each.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: That's right, each. Oh, and a uh Rob Roy. (to Janice) I've always wanted to know...

lines.text: [Scene: Chandler's bedroom, Chandler wakes up, and finds someone else's hand on his chest. He rolls over and is shocked to see Janice there.]

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Happy Valentine's Day!

lines.text: [Scene: The Hallway, Chandler is trying to get Janice out of his apartment.]

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Oh, I miss you already. Can you believe this happened?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: No... no! And yet it did. Good-bye, Janice.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Kiss me!

lines.text: (Janice kisses him. Monica comes out for the newspaper.}

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh, Chandler, sorry.

lines.text: (Janice turns around, Monica sees who it is.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Ohhh, Chandler, sorry! Hey, Janice.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Hi, Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Ok, well, this was very special.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Rach, come see who's out here!

lines.text: (Rachel comes out.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh my god. Janice, hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Janice is gonna go away now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I'll be right back.

lines.text: (Joey enters from the stairs.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh, Joey, look who it is.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (in disbelief) Whoa.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, good, Joey's home now.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: This is so fun. This is like a reunion in the hall.

lines.text: (Monica comes out with her cordless phone.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh, hi, Ross. Yeah. There's someone I want you to say hi to. (to Chandler) He just happened to call.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Hi, Ross. Yes, it's me. How did you know? (she laughs obnoxiously)

lines.text: [Scene: A Chinese Restaurant, Ross is there with his date.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: I'm just sayin' if dogs do experience jet lag, then, because of the whole um, seven dog years to one human year thing, then, when a dog flies from New York to Los Angeles, he doesn't just lose three hours, he loses like a week and a half.

lines.text: (Ross starts to laugh, and then makes a face like 'Why did I just say that?' Ross' ex-wife, Carol, and her lesbian lover, Susan, enter the restaurant. Ross stares at them.)

lines.speaker: Kristin
lines.english: That's funny. Who are they?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: The blond woman is my ex-wife, and the woman touching her is her... close, personal friend.

lines.speaker: Kristin
lines.english: You mean they're lovers.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: If you wanna put a label on it.

lines.speaker: Kristin
lines.english: Wow, uh, anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Nope, nope, that's it.

lines.text: (Carol takes off her jacket, her pregnant belly is exposed.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Oh, and she's pregnant with my baby. I always forget that part. (to Carol and Susan) Helloo!

lines.text: [Scene: Monica and Rachel's, the girls are holding their boyfriend bonfire.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Ok, so now we need, um sage branches and the sacramental wine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: All I have is, is oregano and a Fresca.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Um, that's ok! (throws it in fire) Ok. All right. Now we need the semen of a righteous man.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Ok, Pheebs, you know what, if we had that, we wouldn't be doing the ritual in the first place.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Can we just start throwing things in?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Ok, yeah, ok. (she throws the directions in) Oh, OK.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (tossing things in the fire) Ok, Barry's letters. Adam Ritter's boxer shorts.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Ok, and I have the, uh receipt for my dinner with Nokululu Oon Ah Ah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Look, here's a picture of Scotty Jared naked.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (looking at picture) Hey he's wearing a sweater.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel and Phoebe
lines.english: Eww!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: And here we have the last of Paulo's grappa.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Hey, Rachel, isn't that stuff almost pure...

lines.text: (Rachel throws the alcohol in the fire. A burst of flames shoots up from it.)
[Scene: Central Perk, Chandler and Joey are there. Chandler is preparing to dump Janice again.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: How can I dump this woman on Valentine's day?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: I don't know. You dumped her on New Year's.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, man. In my next life, I'm coming back as a toilet brush.

lines.text: (Janice enters.)

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Hello, funny Valentine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Hi, Just Janice.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Hello, Joey, our little matchmaker. I could just kiss you all over, and I'm gonna!

lines.text: (Janice kisses Joey all over. Chandler smiles.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (to Chandler) If you don't do it, I will.

lines.text: [Scene: The Chinese Restaurant.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: So, um, what do you do for a living?

lines.speaker: Kristin
lines.english: Well, um, for the past few years I've been working..(Ross is watching Carol and Susan, not listening to Kristin. Susan gets up, and has to go. Carol is left stranded)...which is funny because, that wasn't even my major.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.english: Oh no. I thought you said they could shoot the spot without you.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I thought they could...I'll try to get back as soon as I can. I'm sorry. (Ross realizes Kristin was expecting him to laugh, so he starts to laugh hysterically.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Now that is funny. Hey, do you think...would it be too weird if I invited Carol over to join us? 'Cause she's, she's alone now, and pregnant, and, and sad.

lines.speaker: Kristin
lines.english: (reluctantly) I guess.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Are you sure? Great. Carol? Wanna come over and join us?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.english: Oh, no no no. I'm fine. I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Come on. These people'll scooch down. You guys'll scooch, won't you? Let's try scooching! Come on. Come on. Uh, Kristen Riggs, this is Carol Willick. Carol, Kristin. Uh, Carol teaches sixth grade. And, Kristin, Kristin...(struggling)...does something that, funnily enough, wasn't even her major!

lines.text: [Scene: Monica and Rachel's, firemen are there to handle the bonfire that got out of control.]

lines.speaker: Fireman No. 1
lines.english: What do we got there?

lines.speaker: Fireman No. 2
lines.english: A piece of something: boxer shorts, greeting cards, and what looks like a half-charred picture—Wow, that guy's hairier than the Chief!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: You know, it's a really funny story how this happened.

lines.speaker: Fireman No. 3
lines.english: It's all right. It's all right. You don't have to explain. This isn't the first boyfriend bonfire that we've seen get out of control.

lines.speaker: Fireman No. 1
lines.english: You're our third call tonight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Really?

lines.speaker: Fireman No. 2
lines.english: Oh, sure, Valentine's is our busiest night of the year.

lines.text: [Scene: Central Perk.]

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: I brought you something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Is it loaded? Oh, little candy hearts. (reading the candy) Chan and Jan Forever.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: I had them made special.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Ok, Janice. Janice. Hey, Janice. Look, there's no way for me to tell you this. At least there's no new way for me to tell you this. I just don't things are gonna work out.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: That's fine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (surprised) It is?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Mmm-hmm. Because I know that this isn't the end.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh no, you see, actually it is.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: No, it isn't, because you won't let that happen. Don't you know it yet? You love me, Chandler Bing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, no I don't.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: Well then ask yourself this. Why do you think we keep ending up together? New Year's? Who invited who? Valentine's? Who asked who into whose bed?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: I did, but...

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.english: You seek me out. Something deep in your soul calls out to me like a foghorn. Janice, Janice. You want me. You need me. You can't live without me. And you know it. You just don't know you know it. See ya.

lines.text: (She kisses him passionately,then leaves.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Call me!

lines.text: [Scene: The Chinese Restaurant, Ross and Carol are talking. Kristin is not there.]

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.english: It's not true. I never called your mother a wolverine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: You did so. I swear, I swear—(noticing Kristin's absence) How long has she been in the bathroom?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.english: Uh, I don't think she's in the bathroom. Her coat is gone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Well maybe it's cold in there. Or maybe I screwed up the first date I had in 9 years.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.english: That could be it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Oh, god. (He puts his head down on the grill) You know, this is still pretty hot. (He picks his head up, and a mushroom sticks to his head. Carol picks it off and eats it.)

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.english: Mushroom. Smile. They won't all be like this. Some women might even stay through dinner. Sorry, that's not funny

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: No, it's just...you know the whole "getting on with your life" thing. Well, do I have to? I mean, I'm sitting here with this cute woman, and, and, and she's perfectly nice, and, but that there's, that's it. And um, and then I'm here talkin' to you, and, and it's easy, and it's fun, and, and I don't, I don't have to...You know, here's a wacky thought. Um, what's say you and I give it another shot? No no no, I know what you're gonna say, you're a lesbian. But what do you say we just put that aside for now you know? Let's just stick a pin in it, ok? Because, we're great together, you know. You can't deny it. Besides, you're carrying my baby. I mean, how perfect is that? But see, you know, you keep sayin' that, but there's somethin' right here. I love you.

lines.text: (They kiss.)

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.english: Oh, I love you too. But...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: No but, no but.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.english: You know that thing you put over here with the pin in it? It's time to take the pin out. You'll find someone, I know you will. The right woman is just waiting for you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: That's easy for you to say, you found one already.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.english: All you need is a woman who likes men and you'll be set.

lines.text: (A beautiful woman walks by Ross, he stares at her.)

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.english: Not her.

lines.text: [Scene: Monica and Rachel's, The girls are talking with the firemen.]

lines.speaker: Fireman No. 3
lines.english: We get off around midnight, why don't we pick you up then?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: So, um, will you bring the truck?

lines.speaker: Fireman No. 3
lines.english: I'll even let you ring the bell.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh, my god.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: See, there you go, the cleansing works!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: They're nice guys.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh, they're firemen guys.

lines.text: [Scene: Out in the hall, the firemen are talking.]

lines.speaker: Fireman No. 1
lines.english: You guys tell them you were married?

lines.speaker: Fireman No. 2
lines.english: No way!

lines.speaker: Fireman No. 3
lines.english: Are you kidding? My girlfriend doesn't know, I'm not gonna tell them!

lines.text: End
